name: org.machanism.machai:machai-cli
description:
Machai CLI is a Java/Spring Boot + Spring Shell command-line application that provides an interactive shell for the Machanism Machai ecosystem. It focuses on AI-assisted project metadata generation (bindex), metadata registration to an external registry, semantic search of libraries, and AI-driven project assembly, plus Ghostwriter-based code/document scanning and interactive "Act mode" workflows.

Core CLI capabilities:
- Configuration management: persistent CLI defaults are stored in machai.properties via org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.PropertiesConfigurator (see org.machanism.machai.cli.ConfigCommand). Typical defaults include project directory (gw.projectDir), GenAI provider/model identifiers, score thresholds, and logging flags.
- Bindex generation and registration: org.machanism.machai.cli.BindexCommand wraps org.machanism.machai.bindex.BindexCreator (generate bindex metadata files by scanning a project folder) and org.machanism.machai.bindex.BindexRegister (register/upload generated metadata to a registry endpoint). Supports update mode and GenAI model selection.
- Semantic pick and application assembly: org.machanism.machai.cli.AssembyCommand provides "pick" (semantic search using org.machanism.machai.bindex.Picker returning org.machanism.machai.schema.Bindex candidates with similarity scoring) and "assembly" (generate/assemble an output project using org.machanism.machai.bindex.ApplicationAssembly from selected libraries). Inputs can be interactive prompts or file-based prompts; uses a configurable score threshold and GenAI model.
- Ghostwriter guidance pipeline: org.machanism.machai.cli.GWCommand scans directories/files and runs a Ghostwriter guidance processor (org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.GuidanceProcessor / org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.Ghostwriter) with configurable model, system instructions, default guidance prompt, excludes list, concurrency, and optional logging of LLM inputs.
- Act mode: org.machanism.machai.cli.ActCommand runs Ghostwriter "Act mode" via org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.ActProcessor to execute predefined actions/prompts interactively, scanning documents in a configured directory.
- Workspace cleanup: org.machanism.machai.cli.CleanCommand recursively removes Machai temporary folders named .machai from a directory tree.

Runtime and integration details:
- Uses Spring Boot autoconfiguration and Spring Shell commands (@ShellComponent/@ShellMethod) for the user-facing CLI surface.
- Uses SLF4J + Logback configuration to log INFO to console and DEBUG+ to rolling log files under ${machai.home:-.}/logs.
- GenAI integration is abstracted through org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenAIProvider/GenAIProviderManager, with support for tool/function injection via org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionToolsLoader.

Typical usage patterns:
- Configure defaults: set --key gw.projectDir --value ./my-project; set --key gw.model --value OpenAI:gpt-5.1
- Generate and register bindex metadata: bindex --dir ./my-project; register --dir ./my-project --registerUrl https://registry.example/api
- Discover and assemble: pick --query "Create a web app" --score 0.8; assembly --dir ./out
- Run Ghostwriter guidance scan: gw --scanDir ./my-project --excludes target,.git --threads 4 --guidance "Refactor for clarity"
- Run Act mode: act commit; act sonar-fix --model OpenAI:gpt-5.1

This artifact is primarily a CLI application/module in the Machai toolchain that orchestrates bindex and Ghostwriter workflows and calls into other Machai modules (bindex-core, ghostwriter) to perform scanning, semantic search, assembly, and LLM prompting.

constructors.description: Main entry point for starting the Machai CLI. Bootstraps Spring Boot and Spring Shell, optionally loading additional JVM system properties from a machai.properties file (or from a file path specified by the -Dconfig system property) before starting the application.

examples.description: Starts the interactive Machai CLI shell using the default machai.properties if present.

constructors.description: Creates the Ghostwriter guidance command with a JLine LineReader used for interactive prompt input when CLI options are omitted or empty.

examples.snippet:
org.jline.reader.LineReader reader = /* provided by Spring Shell */ null;
org.machanism.machai.cli.GWCommand cmd = new org.machanism.machai.cli.GWCommand(reader);
examples.description: Typical construction is handled by Spring; LineReader is injected lazily for interactive usage.

constructors.description: Creates the semantic-pick and assembly command with an injected JLine LineReader for interactive prompt entry when query is omitted.

examples.snippet:
org.jline.reader.LineReader reader = /* provided by Spring Shell */ null;
org.machanism.machai.cli.AssembyCommand cmd = new org.machanism.machai.cli.AssembyCommand(reader);
examples.description: Spring constructs the command; LineReader is used to ask for a prompt if --query is not supplied.

features.name: gw
features.signature: void gw(int threads, java.lang.String model, java.lang.String instructions, java.lang.String guidance, java.lang.String excludes, java.lang.Boolean logInputs, java.io.File projectDir, java.lang.String[] scanDirs)
features.description: Scans directories or files and runs the Ghostwriter guidance pipeline using the configured GenAI model. Resolves defaults from persistent configuration (machai.properties) via ConfigCommand: project directory, model, instructions, guidance, and logging options. Supports concurrency via --threads, directory exclusion list, optional LLM input logging, and multiple scan directories.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.cli.GWCommand cmd = new org.machanism.machai.cli.GWCommand((org.jline.reader.LineReader) null);
cmd.gw(4, "OpenAI:gpt-5.1", "You are a strict code reviewer", "Refactor for clarity", "target,.git", java.lang.Boolean.TRUE, new java.io.File("."), new java.lang.String[]{"./src"});
examples.description: Runs Ghostwriter guidance processing on ./src using 4 threads and excludes common build/VCS directories.

features.name: act
features.description: Runs Ghostwriter "Act mode" interactively using ActProcessor. The act argument is an array of words composing the action name and optional extra prompt text; they are joined into a single default prompt. Scans a configured directory (gw.scanDir) or falls back to the project directory/current user directory.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.cli.ActCommand cmd = new org.machanism.machai.cli.ActCommand();
cmd.act(new java.lang.String[]{"commit", "and", "push"});
examples.description: Executes the predefined "commit" action with extra prompt text "and push".

features.name: pick
features.signature: void pick(java.lang.String query, java.lang.String registerUrl, java.lang.Double score, java.lang.String model) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Performs semantic search ("pick") for libraries matching a user prompt. Uses org.machanism.machai.bindex.Picker to retrieve candidate org.machanism.machai.schema.Bindex metadata entries above a similarity threshold. The query may be a literal prompt or a file path to a text file containing the prompt. Stores results for subsequent assembly runs.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.cli.AssembyCommand cmd = new org.machanism.machai.cli.AssembyCommand((org.jline.reader.LineReader) null);
cmd.pick("Create a web app", "https://registry.example/api", 0.8d, "OpenAI:gpt-5.1");
examples.description: Finds candidate libraries in the registry matching the prompt, using a minimum similarity score of 0.8.

features.name: assembly
features.signature: void assembly(java.lang.String query, java.io.File dir, java.lang.String registerUrl, java.lang.Double score, java.lang.String model) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Assembles/generates an output project using the currently picked library list (from the last pick) or performs a new pick if a query is provided. Delegates generation to org.machanism.machai.bindex.ApplicationAssembly. Supports configuring output directory, score threshold, registry URL, GenAI model, and optional LLM input logging.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.cli.AssembyCommand cmd = new org.machanism.machai.cli.AssembyCommand((org.jline.reader.LineReader) null);
cmd.assembly("Create a web app", new java.io.File("./out"), "https://registry.example/api", 0.8d, "OpenAI:gpt-5.1");
examples.description: Performs a pick for the prompt and then generates an assembled project into ./out.

features.name: bindex
features.signature: void bindex(java.io.File dir, boolean update, java.lang.String model) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Generates bindex metadata files for a project directory by scanning source and project descriptors. Delegates to org.machanism.machai.bindex.BindexCreator; supports update mode (regenerate/update previously saved metadata) and configurable GenAI model selection.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.cli.BindexCommand cmd = new org.machanism.machai.cli.BindexCommand();
cmd.bindex(new java.io.File("./my-project"), false, "OpenAI:gpt-5.1");
examples.description: Scans ./my-project and generates bindex metadata using the specified model.

features.name: register
features.signature: void register(java.io.File dir, java.lang.String registerUrl, boolean update, java.lang.String model) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Registers (uploads) generated bindex metadata for a project directory into an external registry service. Delegates to org.machanism.machai.bindex.BindexRegister; supports update mode and configurable model selection.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.cli.BindexCommand cmd = new org.machanism.machai.cli.BindexCommand();
cmd.register(new java.io.File("./my-project"), "https://registry.example/api", true, "OpenAI:gpt-5.1");
examples.description: Uploads bindex metadata from ./my-project to the registry, updating existing entries.

features.name: prompt
features.signature: void prompt(java.lang.String query, java.lang.String chatModel, java.io.File dir)
features.description: Sends an ad-hoc user prompt to the configured GenAI provider and prints the response. Applies function/tools to the provider via FunctionToolsLoader and sets the provider working directory. Useful for getting additional AI guidance outside of the scanning/assembly workflows.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.cli.AssembyCommand cmd = new org.machanism.machai.cli.AssembyCommand((org.jline.reader.LineReader) null);
cmd.prompt("Suggest an architecture for a small Spring Boot REST service", "OpenAI:gpt-5.1", new java.io.File("."));
examples.description: Asks the selected GenAI model for architectural guidance with the current directory as working dir.

features.name: clean
features.description: Recursively removes all Machai temporary directories named .machai from a directory tree. Intended to clean intermediate artifacts produced by Machai workflows.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.cli.CleanCommand cmd = new org.machanism.machai.cli.CleanCommand();
cmd.clean(new java.io.File("./my-project"));

features.name: set
features.signature: void set(java.lang.String key, java.lang.String value) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Sets or gets a persistent CLI configuration property stored in machai.properties. If value is null, prints the current property value; otherwise sets and saves it. Used by other commands to resolve defaults for directories, models, thresholds, prompts, and logging behavior.

examples.description: Configures the default GenAI model and default project directory in machai.properties.
